summary: Widen `schema`'s kind domain past the skill/rule fossil to every YAML-frontmatter kind, builtin and declared, with a live-generated unknown-kind error list.

per.section: The placements and their enforcement modes

edit.description: Replace `BUILTIN_DEFAULT_CONTRACT_KINDS` (line 46, currently `["skill", "rule"]`) and both its `Command::Schema` consumers (~279, ~296) with a domain built at dispatch time: every built-in kind from `builtin_kind::definitions()` whose `format` is `Format::YamlFrontmatter` (today: skill, rule, command, agent), plus every declared kind-fact row (`declarations.kinds`, via `compose::partition_kind_rows` the way `read.rs::explain_target` ~1637-1692 already assembles builtin+custom) whose lifted `CustomKind::from_kind_fact_row` format also resolves to `Format::YamlFrontmatter`. `--kind <requested>` looks up this domain (builtin via `compose::builtin_contract`, custom via `compose::default_contract_from_rows` — the same split `read.rs` uses); the unknown-kind error interpolates the live domain instead of the literal string "skill, rule". `--kind` omitted still emits the full by-kind map, now over the widened domain.

Extend `schema_without_kind_maps_every_modeled_kind` (~367) to assert `command` and `agent` also appear in the no-`--kind` map; add a case asserting `--kind command` and `--kind agent` each exit zero with an object schema (mirrors `schema_kind_skill_emits_the_skill_floor_decidable_clauses` ~284); keep `schema_rejects_an_unknown_kind` (~383) green.

tests.asserts: `temper schema --kind command` and `--kind agent` succeed and emit an object schema; `temper schema` with no `--kind` includes skill, rule, command, and agent; an unrecognized kind still hard-errors, now against the live domain.

acceptance: `temper schema --kind <k>` works for every YAML-frontmatter kind in play (builtin: skill/rule/command/agent; any declared kind sharing that format), and the unknown-kind error text is no longer the stale "temper models: skill, rule".
notes: Re-verified 24-07: BUILTIN_DEFAULT_CONTRACT_KINDS (src/main.rs:46) still ["skill","rule"]; error text still says so. YAML-frontmatter builtins today: skill, rule, command, agent (grep confirmed). JSON/TOML kinds (settings-local, plugin-manifest, marketplace, dial) split out to SCHEMA-KEYSTROKE-JSON-TOML-WIRING per the ruling's own scope line. scoped at 162e1cce.

summary: install's scaffold member_dir catch-all buckets 10 of 14 Content::File kinds into one shared "members/" dir with no collision check — same-named command+agent silently overwrite.

edit.description: `member_dir` (~1238) special-cases skill/rule/memory and falls every other kind through `_ => "members"`; replace the catch-all with a per-kind directory (e.g. the bare kind name) so every `Content::File` kind gets its own collision-proof bucket — keep skill/rule/memory's existing directory names as-is (renaming them is its own migration, out of scope here). Both call sites in `scaffold` (~1298 `dir = temper_dir.join(member_dir(kind))`, ~1308 `import_path` format string) ride the same function, no further threading needed.

New regression test: scaffold a harness with two `Content::File` kinds sharing a member id (e.g. a `command` and an `agent` both named "deploy") and assert both scaffold to distinct paths with distinct content — untested today (existing scaffold tests, e.g. ~488, ~523, ~682, exercise skill/rule/memory only).

tests.asserts: A command and an agent sharing a member id scaffold to two distinct files (no silent overwrite); skill/rule/memory scaffold paths are unchanged from today.

acceptance: Every `Content::File` kind's scaffold lands in its own directory; a command/agent (or any two kinds) sharing a member name no longer collide on `write_creating_parents`.
notes: Re-verified 24-07: no src/install.rs commits since 11626a1f; member_dir (line 1238) and its _ => "members" catch-all (1243) unchanged. Decision 0014 keeps command/agent separate identity spaces, so the collision is real. Keep skill/rule/memory's existing directory names — a rename is its own migration, out of scope here. scoped at 162e1cce.

summary: Packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing) and the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json channel (channel 3).

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) is now proven live, not merely wired: the v0.0.11 tag fired it and its publish smoke gate went green (b6dc0a3's commit body).

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json) — distribution.md's v0.1 launch gate: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes.
notes: Park re-tested on disk at 01ba369: release.yml still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim (lines 7-9). The lockstep clause carried here since ac40c72/b676656 is resolved, dropped: da77c60 walked Cargo.toml 0.1.0→0.0.11 to match npm's cadence directly (no v0.1-tag flip), b6dc0a3 fixed the lockfile mismatch, and tag v0.0.11→da77c60 fired release.yml with a green smoke gate per its own commit body — channel 2 is proven live, not just wired. scoped 01ba369.

summary: 0045 also frees guidance onto a field declaration, no predicate — deferred: none of the six promoted built-in notes targets a single field, so there is no consumer yet

gate.reason: 0045's Consequences name field-declaration guidance beside kind-level, but the SDK's only per-field metadata today is a clause's `field:` target (predicate-bound) or `EdgeField` (edge-typed only) — no 'field declaration' list a bare advisory could attach to. None of the six promoted notes (GUIDANCE-BUILTIN-NOTES-PROMOTE) target a single field, inventing the mechanism now is speculative scope with no kind to exercise it ('An export earns its consumer', engineering.md). Revive when a field wants counsel no clause carries.

edit.description: Anticipated: a field-name-keyed guidance fact on `KindFacts`, analogous to `EdgeField`'s field-name-keyed list (196) but for arbitrary fields — shape unset; no field in any built-in kind wants one yet.
Anticipated: a `KindFactRow` column (or sibling row) carrying per-field guidance/cite, following the `to_table`/`from_table` pattern GUIDANCE-KIND-SDK-LOCK-FACT establishes for the kind-level pair.

Anticipated: `emit()`'s per-property `description` (156-172) folds field-declaration guidance in alongside a clause's, the same 'join into one description' discipline `push_description` (316-329) already runs for several guided clauses on one field.
Anticipated: field-level narration extends `narrate_governing_contract` (374-402) or a sibling to print a field's declared guidance.

acceptance: A field declaring `guidance` (no predicate) surfaces it via `schema` hover and `explain`, never gating — provable once a real kind needs one.
notes: 0045 Consequences name field-declaration guidance beside kind-level; this waits for a driver, the same 'no consumer' bar prior deferred entries in this queue's history have held to. scoped at 946e303.

summary: Keystroke JSON Schema wiring for JSON/TOML-document kinds (settings-local, plugin-manifest, marketplace, dial) has no modeline mechanism — deferred out of the YAML-frontmatter widening.

per.section: The placements and their enforcement modes

gate.reason: project_modeline (src/install.rs:1615) reads YAML frontmatter only (frontmatter::frontmatter_matter); a JSON document's native $schema key or a TOML equivalent needs its own design before schema/install extend to those kinds. Ruled (interactive, 2026-07-23) explicitly NOT build's to invent — surface only, never half-invent it. Revive when a JSON/TOML-kind keystroke gap has a real driver.

edit.description: Anticipated: `project_modeline`/`schema_ref` (~1594-1623) grow a JSON-document and/or TOML-document variant once a design exists — a JSON `$schema` top-level key insertion (JSON has no comment channel, so no `MODELINE_MARKER` prefix), a TOML equivalent is unprecedented and needs its own ruling.

acceptance: A JSON- or TOML-document kind (settings-local, plugin-manifest, marketplace, dial, or a declared kind sharing one of those formats) gets keystroke schema validation wired by `install`, once a modeline-equivalent mechanism is designed for its format.
notes: Surfaced by inbox #13 (observed at fc77716) alongside SCHEMA-KIND-DOMAIN-WIDEN; deliberately split so the YAML-frontmatter widening isn't blocked on this open design question. scoped at 162e1cce.
